guides.title: IdeaMark routing guide
guides.description: Discovery metadata for deciding when IdeaMark applies in a v1.2.0 tool chain.

sections.title: IdeaMark scope background
sections.summary: Use IdeaMark when the problem centers on stable, reusable knowledge structure with durable IDs and resolvable local references.

sections.title: IdeaMark non-goals
sections.summary: IdeaMark does not perform retrieval ranking, source dereferencing, storage orchestration, or task-loop execution by itself.

sections.title: Complementary tools
sections.summary: Route iterative task execution to FlowMark first; route stable structural knowledge capture, validation, and projection-driven authoring to IdeaMark.

guides.title: IdeaMark AI authoring guide
guides.description: Guidance for generating valid IdeaMark Core v1.2.0 YAML documents.

sections.title: Projection-first extraction
sections.summary: Read the projection before the source and let it determine reusable entities, occurrences, sections, relations, and perspectives.

sections.title: Reference integrity
sections.summary: Every occurrence must resolve to an entity, every section occurrence must resolve, and local references must remain stable.

guides.title: IdeaMark prompt authoring guide
guides.description: Guidance for building external LLM prompts that preserve v1.2.0 structure and reference validity.

sections.title: Reference mapping rules
sections.summary: Prompts should explicitly require entity, occurrence, section, and relation references to resolve locally.

sections.title: Generation flow
sections.summary: Collect source materials and projection, describe relevant guidance, assemble prompts, generate, validate, repair, and validate strictly.

guides.title: IdeaMark parameter guide
guides.description: Machine-readable and human-readable parameter inventory for v1.2.0 document generation.

sections.title: Core units
sections.summary: The core authoring units are meta, sources, sections, occurrences, entities, relations, perspectives, and optional skeletons.

applies_to: Stabilizing structural knowledge with durable IDs.
Projection-driven reusable knowledge capture from source materials.
Validation, linting, and discovery over local IdeaMark Core v1.2.0 YAML structures.
Skeleton Graph basic-shape discovery when optional skeletons are present.
non_goals: Task-loop orchestration as the primary workflow.
External source dereferencing or truth verification.
Retrieval ranking, storage management, or projection compatibility scoring.

decision_rules.when: The task asks for stable entities, occurrences, sections, local reference validation, or projection-driven extraction.
The task asks for iterative task execution or process orchestration before durable knowledge capture.
